coculture_LD.name: MIT1002 cluster coculture_LD (diel periodic)
coculture_LD.functional_description: Enriched for photosystem I and II components including psbA, psbB, psaA, and psaB. Also includes genes involved in the Calvin cycle, glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis, and glycogen metabolism.
coculture_LD.temporal_pattern: Genes show 24-h periodicity in coculture under diel light:dark cycle but lose periodicity under extended darkness in axenic cultures. Periodicity is maintained longer in coculture during extended darkness.
coculture_LD.confidence_notes: High confidence based on transcriptomic periodicity and pathway enrichment analysis.

supporting_quotes.quote: More Prochlorococcus transcripts exhibited 24-h periodic oscillations in coculture than in pure culture, both over the normal diel cycle and after the shift to extended darkness.

The largest group of Prochlorococcus transcripts (42% of all protein-encoding genes) showed 24-h periodicity in both axenic and cocultures under diel L:D conditions but did not continue to oscillate under extended darkness.

Transcripts associated with a variety of metabolic pathways, including the Calvin cycle, glycolysis, fatty acid biosynthesis, glycogen metabolism, and photosynthesis, continued oscillating under extended darkness in cocultured but not in axenic cells.

coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.name: MIT1002 cluster coculture_LD+coculture_darkness (diel and dark periodic)
coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.functional_description: Includes transcripts involved in organic compound degradation and salvage pathways, such as amino acid degradation and purine nucleotide degradation, indicating mixotrophic metabolism.
coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.temporal_pattern: Genes show 24-h periodicity in coculture under both diel light:dark cycle and extended darkness conditions, maintaining oscillations even in the dark.
coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.expression_dynamics: periodic across all conditions in coculture
coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.enrichment_category: Organic compound metabolism and salvage pathways
coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.confidence_notes: Moderate to high confidence based on differential expression and periodicity analysis.

supporting_quotes.quote: Cocultured, but not axenic, Prochlorococcus cells were enriched in transcripts encoding organic compound degradation/salvage pathways, such as those for amino acids, that could be used by the cell to process organic substrates.
Transcripts for the key enzyme in the Entner-Doudoroff pathway, Eda, were enriched in coculture and consistently depleted in axenic cultures.
Subsets of the Prochlorococcus transcriptome exhibited periodic oscillations under different combinations of culture conditions, including those that oscillated under all conditions.

coculture_LD+coculture_darkness.assessment_notes: Some uncertainty about exact gene membership and pathway enrichment details.

coculture_darkness.name: MIT1002 cluster coculture_darkness (dark periodic)
coculture_darkness.temporal_pattern: Genes do not show significant 24-h periodicity under any condition or only under extended darkness in axenic cultures; specific details not discussed.
coculture_darkness.confidence_notes: No specific discussion or enrichment reported for this cluster.

supporting_quotes.quote: Other transcripts maintained periodic oscillations in different sets of culture conditions, including those that did not exhibit periodicity under any conditions.

coculture_darkness.assessment_notes: No explicit data or discussion available for this cluster.
